items.title: A country divided: state media show Mojtaba supporters as Iranians online fear repression
items.standfirst: With new supreme leader’s strong connections to the IRGC, critics fear worse is to come – if he survivesMiddle East crisis – live updatesAt around midday, even as airstrikes hit several parts of the capital, large crowds gathered in Tehran’s famous Enghelab Square to chant their allegiance to Iran’s new supreme leader.Carrying banners showing the face of the country’s slain leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, people on Monday held a new portrait – that of his son and successor, Mojtaba Khamenei. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran’s new supreme leader brings mystery element to Middle East crisis
items.standfirst: What Mojtaba Khamenei will do with his leadership is now the key question after he succeeds his fatherMiddle East crisis – live updatesCrowds in Tehran greeted the announcement of the country’s new supreme leader by chanting: “God’s hand is still upon us, Khamenei is still our leader.” As the world economy grinds to a halt, Iran is selling the elevation of Mojtaba Khamenei as a sign of reassuring continuity for a country determined to show its defiance of the west.Yet in reality he injects a new unpredictable, even mysterious, element into the Middle East crisis, since just as he is unknown to Washington, so he is a figure of deep obscurity to ordinary Iranians. By contrast, the first supreme leader, Ruhollah Khomeini, led Iran to revolution in 1979 and the second, Mojtaba’s father, Ali Khamenei, had been president for eight years before he was chosen by the Assembly of Experts within a day of Khomenei’s death. Continue reading...
items.content: What Mojtaba Khamenei will do with his leadership is now the key question after he succeeds his fatherMiddle East crisis – live updatesCrowds in Tehran greeted the announcement of the country’s new supreme leader by chanting: “God’s hand is still upon us, Khamenei is still our leader.” As the world economy grinds to a halt, Iran is selling the elevation of Mojtaba Khamenei as a sign of reassuring continuity for a country determined to show its defiance of the west.Yet in reality he injects a new unpredictable, even mysterious, element into the Middle East crisis, since just as he is unknown to Washington, so he is a figure of deep obscurity to ordinary Iranians. By contrast, the first supreme leader, Ruhollah Khomeini, led Iran to revolution in 1979 and the second, Mojtaba’s father, Ali Khamenei, had been president for eight years before he was chosen by the Assembly of Experts within a day of Khomenei’s death. Continue reading...

items.title: US and Israel launch strikes across Iran as crowds celebrate new supreme leader
items.standfirst: Mojtaba Khamenei’s supporters take to streets of Tehran on the same day Iran attacks targets in Israel and Gulf statesMiddle East crisis – live updatesUS and Israeli warplanes launched new waves of strikes on targets across Iran on Monday, as large crowds took to the streets in Tehran in a defiant show of support for Mojtaba Khamenei, the country’s newly appointed supreme leader.The conflict, now in its second week, continued to escalate, with fresh Iranian missile and drone attacks targeting Israel, US bases across the Middle East and energy infrastructure in the Gulf. Continue reading...

items.title: What to know about Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader
items.standfirst: The second son of the late supreme leader keeps a low profile. But he's long been viewed as wielding his power behind the scenes, from crushing dissent to influencing presidential elections.
items.content: The second son of the late supreme leader keeps a low profile. But he's long been viewed as wielding his power behind the scenes, from crushing dissent to influencing presidential elections.(Image credit: Atta Kenare)

items.title: The rise of Mojtaba Khamenei
items.standfirst: Iran’s new supreme leader has been shaped by decades-long ties with the powerful and hardline Revolutionary Guards
items.content: Iran’s new supreme leader has been shaped by decades-long ties with the powerful and hardline Revolutionary Guards

items.title: What will Iran's new Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei be in charge of?
items.standfirst: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has become Iran's new supreme leader. The secretive 56-year-old cleric is only the third supreme leader in the history of the Islamic Republic and has close ties to the paramilitary Revolutionary Guard. But what will his role actually entail?
items.content: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has become Iran's new supreme leader. The secretive 56-year-old cleric is only the third supreme leader in the history of the Islamic Republic and has close ties to the paramilitary Revolutionary Guard. But what will his role actually entail?

items.title: Trump trying to gauge whether new ayatollah is 'a leader he can work with'
items.standfirst: Iran's council of 88 clerics appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late Ayatallah Ali Khamenei, as new supreme leader of the country. This as US-Israeli strikes keep pounding the country, targeting oil, civilian and military infrastructure. Now, US President Donald Trump is 'gauging' whether the new ayatollah is 'a leader he can work with', Nega Angha, Visiting Fellow at King's College London, tells FRANCE 24.
items.content: Iran's council of 88 clerics appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late Ayatallah Ali Khamenei, as new supreme leader of the country. This as US-Israeli strikes keep pounding the country, targeting oil, civilian and military infrastructure. Now, US President Donald Trump is 'gauging' whether the new ayatollah is 'a leader he can work with', Nega Angha, Visiting Fellow at King's College London, tells FRANCE 24.

items.title: Khamenei replaces Khamenei: Iran defies Trump, signals continuity
items.standfirst: Iran’s choice of Mojtaba Khamenei to replace his slain father, Ali Khamenei, as supreme leader signals the entrenchment of hardline cleric power and a continued resistance amid the US-Israeli military onslaught. It also underscores US President Donald Trump’s policy failure to detail the goals of the war.
items.content: Iran’s choice of Mojtaba Khamenei to replace his slain father, Ali Khamenei, as supreme leader signals the entrenchment of hardline cleric power and a continued resistance amid the US-Israeli military onslaught. It also underscores US President Donald Trump’s policy failure to detail the goals of the war.

clusters.title: How high could oil go, and what might the global economic fallout be?
clusters.neutral_headline: How high could oil go, and what might the global economic fallout be
bullet_summary: Following a remote meeting on Monday, G7 finance ministers said they would closely monitor the situation but stopped short of agreeing to release emergency oil reserves
Reported by Guardian World, Financial Times, BBC World and others

items.title: How high could oil go, and what might the global economic fallout be?
items.standfirst: There is talk of crude surpassing its record high of 2008 with potentially dire effects for consumers and businessesFears over the global economy have been stoked by the oil price soaring past $100 a barrel as a result on the US-Israel war with Iran.Economists say the increasing likelihood of a prolonged conflict in the vital energy exporting region could have serious consequences for living standards around the world amid the threat of a renewed inflation shock. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: UK inflation likely to rise because of Middle East war, says Rachel Reeves
items.standfirst: British chancellor says she will take steps to help families with cost of living as oil prices surge Middle East crisis – live updatesBritain is likely to be hit by rising inflation because of the US war with Iran, the UK chancellor has said, as she suggested that a “rapid de-escalation” would be the best protection against a jump in energy prices.Both Rachel Reeves and the prime minister, Keir Starmer, suggested the government would be prepared to intervene to protect UK households against major cost-of-living shocks as oil prices surged past $100 (£75) a barrel for the first time since 2022. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: G7 ready to take ‘necessary measures’ over economic impact of Iran war
items.standfirst: Finance ministers monitoring situation but stop short of agreeing to release emergency oil reservesIran war drives oil prices above $100 a barrelBusiness live – latest updatesThe G7 said it was ready to take “necessary measures” to address the economic impact of the US-Israel war on Iran, after a meeting prompted by soaring oil prices, which rose above $100 (£74) a barrel for the first time since 2022.Following a remote meeting on Monday, G7 finance ministers said they would closely monitor the situation but stopped short of agreeing to release emergency oil reserves. Continue reading...
items.content: Finance ministers monitoring situation but stop short of agreeing to release emergency oil reservesIran war drives oil prices above $100 a barrelBusiness live – latest updatesThe G7 said it was ready to take “necessary measures” to address the economic impact of the US-Israel war on Iran, after a meeting prompted by soaring oil prices, which rose above $100 (£74) a barrel for the first time since 2022.Following a remote meeting on Monday, G7 finance ministers said they would closely monitor the situation but stopped short of agreeing to release emergency oil reserves. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran war drives oil prices above $100 a barrel for first time since 2022
items.standfirst: Trump says rise in energy prices is ‘very small price to pay’ as conflict sparks fresh stock market sell-offBusiness live – latest updatesOil prices surged past $100 a barrel for the first time since 2022 as fallout from the US-Israel war with Iran continued to rattle global markets and leading economies moved to tackle a worsening energy supply crisis.A weekend of escalating violence in the Middle East intensified concerns around a sustained supply crunch, propelling oil prices to their highest level in four years and triggering a deep stock market sell-off. Continue reading...
items.content: Trump says rise in energy prices is ‘very small price to pay’ as conflict sparks fresh stock market sell-offBusiness live – latest updatesOil prices surged past $100 a barrel for the first time since 2022 as fallout from the US-Israel war with Iran continued to rattle global markets and leading economies moved to tackle a worsening energy supply crisis.A weekend of escalating violence in the Middle East intensified concerns around a sustained supply crunch, propelling oil prices to their highest level in four years and triggering a deep stock market sell-off. Continue reading...

items.title: Why has the Iran war sparked fears of stagflation for the global economy?
items.standfirst: With oil prices soaring and stock markets falling, economists warn that a prolonged conflict in the Middle East risks knocking growth worldwide and boosting pricesMiddle East crisis - live updatesOil prices continued to surge on Monday, triggering a stark sell-off across some of the world’s leading stock markets amid growing concern that the US-Israel war on Iran could set the stage for a global economic shock.The Middle East conflict has sparked an energy supply crisis that could risk driving up inflation and interest rates, according to economists, who believe growth is set to weaken while prices rise. Fears of stagflation – where economic activity stagnates, but inflation increases – loom large. Continue reading...
items.content: With oil prices soaring and stock markets falling, economists warn that a prolonged conflict in the Middle East risks knocking growth worldwide and boosting pricesMiddle East crisis - live updatesOil prices continued to surge on Monday, triggering a stark sell-off across some of the world’s leading stock markets amid growing concern that the US-Israel war on Iran could set the stage for a global economic shock.The Middle East conflict has sparked an energy supply crisis that could risk driving up inflation and interest rates, according to economists, who believe growth is set to weaken while prices rise. Fears of stagflation – where economic activity stagnates, but inflation increases – loom large. Continue reading...

items.title: Why an Iran war inflation shock could wreck global economic recovery
items.standfirst: Central bankers and economists warn prolonged conflict could raise retail prices and rip up growth forecastsHeather Stewart: UK must be prepared for a price shock from the Iran warAn inflation shock triggered by the US-Israel attack on Iran could wreck a fragile global economic recovery that had been expected to gain momentum this year.With oil and gas prices spiking, despite a pledge from Donald Trump to protect tankers making their way through the crucial strait of Hormuz shipping chokepoint, central bankers and economists have warned that a prolonged conflict could increase retail prices around the world and force them to rip up growth forecasts for this year. Continue reading...
items.content: Central bankers and economists warn prolonged conflict could raise retail prices and rip up growth forecastsHeather Stewart: UK must be prepared for a price shock from the Iran warAn inflation shock triggered by the US-Israel attack on Iran could wreck a fragile global economic recovery that had been expected to gain momentum this year.With oil and gas prices spiking, despite a pledge from Donald Trump to protect tankers making their way through the crucial strait of Hormuz shipping chokepoint, central bankers and economists have warned that a prolonged conflict could increase retail prices around the world and force them to rip up growth forecasts for this year. Continue reading...

clusters.neutral_headline: Live Nation to open Ticketmaster to other sellers in U.S. antitrust deal
bullet_summary: MacFarlane is only the latest departure from the Bari Weiss-led network, though he said in a memo to colleagues – also posted on LinkedIn – that he personally made the decision to leave
The settlement was announced during a court hearing Monday morning

items.title: Live Nation to open Ticketmaster to other sellers in U.S. antitrust deal
items.standfirst: Live Nation, the parent company of Ticketmaster, has reached a deal with the Department of Justice as part of a high-stakes antitrust trial.
items.content: Live Nation, the parent company of Ticketmaster, has reached a deal with the Department of Justice as part of a high-stakes antitrust trial.

items.title: Live Nation to open Ticketmaster to other sellers as part of $280 million settlement, sources say
items.standfirst: Live Nation, the parent company of Ticketmaster, has agreed to pay $280 million in civil penalties to 40 states that sued the company on antitrust grounds. As part of the settlement, Ticketmaster must also open technology to allow other ticket sellers to use its platform to reach customers, multiple sources told CBS News. Yasmin Khorram, economic policy reporter for Politico, joined CBS News to discuss.
items.content: Live Nation, the parent company of Ticketmaster, has agreed to pay $280 million in civil penalties to 40 states that sued the company on antitrust grounds. As part of the settlement, Ticketmaster must also open technology to allow other ticket sellers to use its platform to reach customers, multiple sources told CBS News. Yasmin Khorram, economic policy reporter for Politico, joined CBS News to discuss.

items.title: What changes did Live Nation agree to? #entertainment
items.standfirst: Settlement outcome and its implications Live Nation reached a settlement with the U.S. Department of Justice that resolves the federal antitrust case the company faced. As part of the agreement, the company committed to implement "structural changes" aimed at addressing the competitive concerns…

items.title: CBS News’ Scott MacFarlane leaves network: ‘I look forward to some independence’
items.standfirst: Justice correspondent most known for his January 6 Capitol riot coverage, is latest to quit Bari Weiss-led networkSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxScott MacFarlane, the CBS News justice correspondent most known for his extensive coverage of the January 6 Capitol insurrection, surprised colleagues on Monday morning by announcing his departure from the network, which he joined in late 2021.MacFarlane is only the latest departure from the Bari Weiss-led network, though he said in a memo to colleagues – also posted on LinkedIn – that he personally made the decision to leave. Continue reading...

items.title: Live Nation reaches surprise settlement with justice department in antitrust case
items.standfirst: Live Nation will pay $200m to states in lawsuit, and Ticketmaster will open parts of platform to rival companiesSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxLive Nation, which owns Ticketmaster, has reached a surprise settlement with the Department of Justice in its antitrust case just one week after the trial began.The settlement was announced during a court hearing Monday morning. Under the agreement, Live Nation will pay roughly $200m in damages to states that participated in the lawsuit, and Ticketmaster will be required to open parts of its platform to rival ticketing companies, reported Politico. Continue reading...
items.content: Live Nation will pay $200m to states in lawsuit, and Ticketmaster will open parts of platform to rival companiesSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxLive Nation, which owns Ticketmaster, has reached a surprise settlement with the Department of Justice in its antitrust case just one week after the trial began.The settlement was announced during a court hearing Monday morning. Under the agreement, Live Nation will pay roughly $200m in damages to states that participated in the lawsuit, and Ticketmaster will be required to open parts of its platform to rival ticketing companies, reported Politico. clusters.title: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges
clusters.neutral_headline: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, France24, Financial Times

items.title: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges
items.standfirst: Ekrem Imamoglu is seen as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.
items.content: Ekrem Imamoglu is seen as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.

items.title: Jailed Istanbul mayor goes on trial in corruption case slammed as political
items.standfirst: Istanbul's former mayor Ekrem Imamoglu has gone on trial in Turkey on charges of corruption, in a landmark case widely criticised by rights groups. Imamoglu was arrested almost one year ago, when the opposition CHP party named him as their presidential candidate. Critics say the trial is an attempt to scupper Imamoglu's chances of beating Recep Tayyip Erdogan at the ballot box.
items.content: Istanbul's former mayor Ekrem Imamoglu has gone on trial in Turkey on charges of corruption, in a landmark case widely criticised by rights groups. Imamoglu was arrested almost one year ago, when the opposition CHP party named him as their presidential candidate. Critics say the trial is an attempt to scupper Imamoglu's chances of beating Recep Tayyip Erdogan at the ballot box.

items.title: Landmark trial begins for Turkish opposition leader Imamoglu
items.standfirst: Istanbul's jailed mayor Ekrem Imamoglu went on trial on Monday in a widespread corruption case which critics say is a politically motivated bid to eliminate his chances of challenging President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.
items.content: Istanbul's jailed mayor Ekrem Imamoglu went on trial on Monday in a widespread corruption case which critics say is a politically motivated bid to eliminate his chances of challenging President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

clusters.title: Macron says France, allies preparing 'defensive' mission to reopen Strait of Hormuz
clusters.neutral_headline: France preparing to escort ships in Strait of Hormuz when war calms: Macron
bullet_summary: France and its allies are preparing a "defensive" mission to reopen the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, President Emmanuel Macron said Monday as the Middle East war entered its second week
President Emmanuel Macron said on a visit to Cyprus on Monday that France and its allies were working to put together a "purely defensive" mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
"When Cyprus is attacked it is Europe that is attacked" said French President Emmanuel Macron as he spoke during a visit to Cyprus after it was targeted by Iranian-made drones

items.title: Macron says France, allies preparing 'defensive' mission to reopen Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: France and its allies are preparing a "defensive" mission to reopen the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, President Emmanuel Macron said Monday as the Middle East war entered its second week. The French leader landed by helicopter on the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, dispatched to the Mediterranean after US-Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28 triggered a war that has sown chaos in the Middle East and threatens to spill into other regions.
items.content: France and its allies are preparing a "defensive" mission to reopen the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, President Emmanuel Macron said Monday as the Middle East war entered its second week. The French leader landed by helicopter on the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, dispatched to the Mediterranean after US-Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28 triggered a war that has sown chaos in the Middle East and threatens to spill into other regions.

items.title: Europe showing it is 'prepared but does not want to be dragged into a war it wasn't consulted on'
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron is visiting the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, currently off the coast of Crete and heading towards Cyprus. It had been dispatched days before after the island was targeted by a Shahed drone-attack. FRANCE 24 's Kethevane Gorjestani looks at how Europe is reacting to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron is visiting the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, currently off the coast of Crete and heading towards Cyprus. It had been dispatched days before after the island was targeted by a Shahed drone-attack. FRANCE 24 's Kethevane Gorjestani looks at how Europe is reacting to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Replay: Macron addresses French troops as naval vessels are deployed to the Mediterranean
items.standfirst: Rewatch as French President Emmanuel Macron addresses troops on the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier as it is deployed in the eastern Mediterranean in an 'unprecedented' move.
items.content: Rewatch as French President Emmanuel Macron addresses troops on the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier as it is deployed in the eastern Mediterranean in an 'unprecedented' move.

items.title: France preparing to escort ships in Strait of Hormuz when war calms: Macron
items.standfirst: French president says 'purely defensive' mission aims to gradually reopen key Gulf waterway amid spike in oil prices.
items.content: French president says 'purely defensive' mission aims to gradually reopen key Gulf waterway amid spike in oil prices.

items.title: Macron announces ‘purely defensive’ mission to reopen Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: President Emmanuel Macron said on a visit to Cyprus on Monday that France and its allies were working to put together a "purely defensive" mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. He said the mission would be aimed at escorting vessels "after the end of the hottest phase of the conflict" in the Middle East to ensure the flow of oil and gas – as the war pitting the US and Israel against Iran entered its second week, sending global oil prices soaring.
items.content: President Emmanuel Macron said on a visit to Cyprus on Monday that France and its allies were working to put together a "purely defensive" mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. He said the mission would be aimed at escorting vessels "after the end of the hottest phase of the conflict" in the Middle East to ensure the flow of oil and gas – as the war pitting the US and Israel against Iran entered its second week, sending global oil prices soaring.

items.title: 'This is all about defence': Macron addresses European security in Cyprus
items.standfirst: "When Cyprus is attacked it is Europe that is attacked" said French President Emmanuel Macron as he spoke during a visit to Cyprus after it was targeted by Iranian-made drones. He also mentioned that France and its partners are preparing a 'purely defensive' mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more about Europe's reaction to the conflict in the Middle East.
items.content: "When Cyprus is attacked it is Europe that is attacked" said French President Emmanuel Macron as he spoke during a visit to Cyprus after it was targeted by Iranian-made drones. He also mentioned that France and its partners are preparing a 'purely defensive' mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more about Europe's reaction to the conflict in the Middle East.

items.standfirst: Rewatch as French President Emmanuel Macron addresses the threat posed by Iran to Cyprus, an EU member.
items.content: Rewatch as French President Emmanuel Macron addresses the threat posed by Iran to Cyprus, an EU member.

items.title: Ex-Mail on Sunday journalist denies ordering ‘blag’ of Sadie Frost’s medical information
items.standfirst: Katie Nicholl’s name appears on many of the stories that Frost, Prince Harry and others have complained aboutA senior former Mail on Sunday journalist has denied commissioning a “blag” of sensitive medical information about Sadie Frost that the actor had not even told her own mother.At the high court, Katie Nicholl, a former diary editor and royal editor at the paper, was accused of using blagged information from a private investigator to uncover “extraordinarily intrusive” details of Frost’s medical history. Continue reading...

items.title: Markets plunge as price of oil reaches 4-year high
items.standfirst: Stock markets plunged on Monday as the price of oil hit more than $100 per barrel for the first time since July 2022. Noam Raydan, a senior fellow at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy, joins with more.
items.content: Stock markets plunged on Monday as the price of oil hit more than $100 per barrel for the first time since July 2022. Noam Raydan, a senior fellow at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy, joins with more.

items.title: Oil hits $100 per barrel for first time since July 2022
items.standfirst: The price of oil surged past $100 on Sunday for the first time since July 2022 as the ripple effects from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran continued to hit global markets.
items.content: The price of oil surged past $100 on Sunday for the first time since July 2022 as the ripple effects from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran continued to hit global markets.

clusters.title: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
clusters.neutral_headline: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
items.standfirst: Shah’s Rastriya Swatantra party secures thumping victory in first poll since gen Z protests that toppled government Balendra Shah, the rapper turned politician and popular figurehead of a gen Z revolution, looks set to become Nepal’s next prime minister after his party won by an unprecedented margin.Shah, known widely as Balen, and his Rastriya Swatantra party (RSP) secured a rare landslide victory in the first election since youth-led protests during which dozens were killed and the former government was toppled. Continue reading...
items.content: Shah’s Rastriya Swatantra party secures thumping victory in first poll since gen Z protests that toppled government Balendra Shah, the rapper turned politician and popular figurehead of a gen Z revolution, looks set to become Nepal’s next prime minister after his party won by an unprecedented margin.Shah, known widely as Balen, and his Rastriya Swatantra party (RSP) secured a rare landslide victory in the first election since youth-led protests during which dozens were killed and the former government was toppled. Continue reading...

items.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah unseats Nepal's ex-PM as he heads for victory
items.standfirst: Thursday's general election was the first since violent youth-led protests toppled the government in September.
items.content: Thursday's general election was the first since violent youth-led protests toppled the government in September.
